EXCEPTION: If the user has already provided an unambiguous, explicit authorization in the conversation to update the specified order address(es) (for example: the user explicitly says 'Please update my account and both orders to X address'), the assistant may proceed without an additional get_order_details/confirmation step.
IF find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) returns no user → THEN ask the user for their email and call find_user_id_by_email(email) to attempt to locate the account as a fallback.

IF an order's status is cancelled → THEN the order cannot be reinstated via automated return/modify/cancel/exchange tools; refuse automated reinstatement and escalate to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents) for manual review/possible reinstatement.

IF a user provides a customer ID / user_id directly → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve the user's email, payment methods, address, and list of order IDs. Accept the provided user_id as valid identifying info in lieu of name+zip or email and DO NOT prompt for additional identifying information (name/zip/email) or repeat other lookups unless the user explicitly indicates they want to use a different account or believes the provided user_id is incorrect. If inconsistent identifying info is later provided and the user requests switching accounts, confirm explicitly before performing any account lookup or action on the alternate account.

IF a user requests to split payment across multiple payment methods for a single order at the time of purchase → THEN refuse the request and inform the user that a single payment method must be used for that order; offer alternatives such as removing items to reduce the total, using a different single payment method, or placing a new separate order to use a different card. EXCEPTION: for order exchanges or modifications that create a price difference (additional charge or refund), it is permitted to apply the price difference to a different payment method than the original order (resulting in multiple payment entries in the order's payment_history). In those cases follow precondition rules for selecting and validating the payment_method_id and obtain explicit user confirmation before charging a different payment method unless the user already provided a clear authorization.

IF an order status is pending AND the user requests cancellation of only some items in that pending order → THEN do not perform partial cancellations; pending orders may be modified (change variants/quantities) using modify_pending_order_items or cancelled in full using cancel_pending_order. Partial-item returns/cancellations are only allowed for delivered orders.

IF the user requests multiple returns/exchanges across multiple orders → THEN compute and clearly present the aggregated total refund or total charge (net) and list which payment method(s) each amount will be applied to (e.g., 'Total refund would be $X to your [payment method(s)]'), and confirm with the user.

IF a user requests an aggregate total or spending summary for a product type/category across their past orders → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain order IDs, call get_order_details(order_id) for each order, filter items by the requested product_id(s) or product name, sum the item prices, and present the total with an optional breakdown by order and item.

IF multiple exchanges/returns/modifications are requested across one or more orders → THEN present a per-order breakdown listing order_id, old item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or return/cancel), per-item and per-order price(s), the price difference for each order, the payment_method_id to be used for each charge/refund, and the total net charge or refund across all orders; require explicit user confirmation before executing any tool calls. EXCEPTION: If the assistant provides a clear, unambiguous summary of the proposed replacements/changes and the user explicitly confirms (for example 'Please proceed' or equivalent), the assistant may proceed without repeating a full per-order line-item breakdown.

DO NOT request identifying information (name/zip/email) for general product inquiries, browsing, availability requests, or unrelated/entertainment questions (for example: poems, riddles, casual conversation). If the user's intent is ambiguous, first ask a short clarifying question to determine whether they are requesting an account-specific action; only prompt for identifying info when the user explicitly requests account-specific actions (account/order changes, returns, exchanges, cancellations, or other actions that require accessing the user's account or orders).

IF an order already has an active return_requested or exchange_requested state → THEN do not initiate additional return or exchange requests for the same order until the existing request is resolved; inform the user of the existing request and next steps.

IF a payment_method of type gift_card is used for a modification, pending-order charge, OR for a refund/return → THEN: (a) check and report the gift_card's available balance from get_user_details/payment method metadata before attempting to charge it; if the balance is insufficient to cover any required charge, DO NOT execute the exchange/modification and follow the shortfall process in prec-007; (b) when a refund is applied to a gift_card, update the gift_card balance appropriately (deduct for charges, add for refunds) in the system and include the updated gift card balance in the confirmation communication after the action is prepared and/or executed; (c) always state the expected standard processing timeframe for gift-card refunds/charges (e.g., 'refund will take X–Y business days') in the confirmation; and (d) report monetary amounts rounded to two decimal places and the payment_method_id used for the charge/refund. For charges, verify available balance per prec-007 before executing; for refunds, apply the refund to the gift card balance and include the updated balance and expected processing timeframe in the confirmation.

IF a user requests cancellation for only part of a pending order → THEN inform the user that partial cancellations are not supported (or per-system policy), and offer the appropriate alternative (use modify_pending_order_items to remove/replace items if supported, or cancel the entire order if the system supports it).

IF an address modification request is received → THEN check the order status: delivered orders' shipping addresses cannot be changed; only pending/processed (modifiable) orders can have their shipping address updated. Inform the user which orders cannot be changed and proceed only for eligible orders.

IF a user asks a question or makes a request that is unrelated to their account, orders, returns, or exchanges → THEN do not request identifying information (name/zip/email). Only request identifying info when it is needed to look up or act on the user's account/orders.

IF a user is unsure which payment method they used for an order → THEN always retrieve the relevant order_details and read payment_history to identify the original payment_method_id to use for refunds/charges before asking the user for a payment method. If the order_details include a usable payment_method_id, do not prompt the user for a payment method; instead state the identified original payment method to the user (for example: 'the original payment method was your Mastercard ending in 2478') and use it by default unless the user explicitly requests a different payment method. Only ask the user to provide or choose a payment method if the order details do not contain usable payment information or if the user explicitly requests a different payment method and the system permits it.

IF a user requests refunds/returns be applied to a different payment method than the original order's payment_history (including requests for refunds on delivered items) → THEN: By default refund to the original payment method recorded in the order's payment_history and refuse requests to route such refunds to payment methods not in the user's account or to another customer's method. EXCEPTION: For exchanges or price-difference adjustments (including delivered-order exchanges and pending-order modifications) the customer may explicitly request that any charge or refund related to the exchange/modification be applied to a different payment method in their account; in those cases verify the requested payment_method_id belongs to the user (per prec-003) and, if the system supports it, honor the request and include payment_method_id in the tool call. Do not call return_delivered_order_items (or any refund tool) using an alternate payment method for standard returns of delivered items. If the requested alternate payment method cannot be used (for example, the original payment was a gift card) clearly explain why the refund cannot be applied to the requested method, present practical alternatives — (1) refund the specified returned item(s) to the original payment method only; (2) return the entire order so the full refund is processed to the original payment method; (3) perform an exchange or modification if that flow permits applying charges/refunds to a different payment method (verify ownership of that method per prec-003) and, if supported, apply the requested method; or (4) offer to escalate to human agents for review — and refuse any attempt to use a payment method not in the user's account while offering escalation. Require explicit user confirmation before proceeding with any chosen alternative; when the user selects an alternative, summarize the exact actions, the payment_method_id(s) that will be used, and any charges or refunds (rounded to cents) and obtain confirmation before calling the relevant tool(s).

IF requested returns/exchanges include items from more than one order → THEN handle each order separately: call get_order_details(order_id) for each order involved, and for each order call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items using the payment_method_id from that order's payment_history for refunds/charges, ensuring each tool call references the correct order and payment method.
IF a user provides only a single name (for example, only a first name) together with a zip code → THEN ask for the full name (first and last) before attempting a name+zip lookup.

IF a user requests an action that is disallowed by policy (for example, refunds to a different payment method) OR explicitly requests a human agent or escalation → THEN refuse the disallowed action per policy and offer/escalate to human agents using transfer_to_human_agents(summary) when the user requests it or when appropriate.

IF cancelling a pending order → THEN ask for the cancellation reason (e.g., 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') prior to cancelling. A clear user instruction with a stated reason may be treated as confirmation to proceed with the cancellation.

If a user asks how many product options/variants are currently available for a product or product type (for example, "how many t-shirt options are available?"), identify the product_id (use list_all_product_types if needed), call get_product_details(product_id), count the variants where available == true, and report that count to the user; optionally include a short summary of the available variants (key option attributes and prices) or how to view them. Do not request identifying information for this general product inquiry.

DO NOT guarantee expedited refund processing outside the standard stated processing window. If a user requests a faster refund timeframe than standard (for example, 'within 3 days'), state the standard processing timeframe (e.g., 'refunds typically take 5–7 business days'), explain that the assistant cannot ensure an accelerated processing time, and offer to escalate to human agents for review if the customer insists or requests further assistance (use transfer_to_human_agents(summary)).

IF a user requests updating or modifying the same product across all of their pending orders (for example: 'update all my pending t-shirt orders to X') → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain order IDs, call get_order_details(order_id) for each order, filter for orders with status 'pending' that contain the specified product_id or product name, prepare a per-order proposal listing order_id, current item_id(s) to be changed, proposed new_item_id, per-item price and any price differences, and the payment_method_id that will be charged/refunded for that order (use that order's original payment method). Present the aggregated proposal and require explicit user confirmation before executing modify_pending_order_items for each eligible pending order. Do not modify non-pending orders and inform the user which orders (if any) were ineligible.

IF a user returns delivered items (initiates a return/refund) → THEN inform the user of the expected refund processing time (for example, 'refund will take X–Y business days') in the confirmation communication along with the amount and the payment method that will receive the refund.

IF an order contains multiple identical items represented by duplicate item_ids AND the user requests exchanges/returns affecting one or more units → THEN treat each unit as a separate item by including the item_id multiple times in tool calls (e.g., ["id","id"]) to indicate quantity. If the user references units ambiguously (for example: 'one of them' or 'both'), ask the user to specify which units or to confirm the number of units to act on before proceeding. Ensure get_order_details has been called to confirm item_ids and quantities before any exchange/return tool calls.

IF a user requests cancelling all (or multiple) pending orders at once → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) and get_order_details(order_id) for each order to identify which orders are currently in 'pending' status. Present the list of pending orders (order_id and items) to the user and ask whether they want to cancel all pending orders or select specific ones, and request a cancellation reason (a single reason can be applied to multiple orders). After explicit user confirmation and reason, call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) for each pending order selected, report which orders were successfully cancelled, and provide refund details (payment_method used and amounts). If the user did not provide a reason, ask for one prior to cancelling. If some orders are not pending, inform the user those cannot be cancelled and offer alternatives.

IF a user requests the "cheapest" variant matching specific criteria (for example: "cheapest i7 or better", "least expensive with 8GB RAM", etc.) → THEN call list_all_product_types and get_product_details(product_id), filter the product's variants to only those where available == true and whose options meet the user's specified criteria, select the matching variant(s) with the lowest price, and present the chosen variant(s) to the user including item_id, full options, availability, and price for explicit confirmation before proceeding. If multiple variants tie for lowest price, present all tied options and ask the user to choose.

IF a user requests lowering a pending order's total to a target amount by swapping item variants → THEN for each item call get_product_details(product_id) to identify available (available == true) variants and select the lowest-priced available variant; propose replacements only for items where a cheaper available variant exists; compute the new per-item prices, per-order total, and the net refund or charge; present a clear per-item and per-order breakdown (order_id, old item_ids and prices, proposed new_item_ids and prices, net price difference, and the payment_method_id that will be used) and ask for explicit confirmation before calling modify_pending_order_items. If the cheapest available replacements cannot meet the user's target total, inform the user and offer alternatives (remove items, choose different variants, or cancel the order).

IF a user requests a return/refund but indicates they do not have the physical item (lost/stolen/missing) → THEN refuse to process an automated return because returns require possession of the item. Explain that an automated return cannot be completed without the item, provide the order's tracking information if available, and offer alternatives: (a) escalate/file a claim for investigation with human agents (use transfer_to_human_agents(summary)) if the user requests further review, (b) place a new order if the user wants a replacement, or (c) advise on next steps for recovery with the carrier. If the user insists on a refund despite the item being missing, offer to escalate to human agents for manual review rather than performing an automated refund.

IF a user requests returns/exchanges that include items belonging to orders with status 'cancelled' → THEN do not attempt automated returns/exchanges for those cancelled items. Inform the user which specific items and order_id(s) are cancelled and cannot be returned, proceed with return/exchange actions only for eligible items from other orders (call get_order_details for each order and use the appropriate return/exchange tool per order), and offer to escalate/transfer to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents(summary)) if the user requests review or possible reinstatement of cancelled orders/items.

IF an exchange requests replacement of a delivered item with the same item_id (a like-for-like replacement) → THEN allow exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id) where new_item_ids equal the original item_ids, provided get_product_details confirms the variant is available (or availability is not applicable for direct replacements), there is no existing active return_requested or exchange_requested for the same order, and the user has explicitly requested the replacement. Treat this as a standard exchange/replacement flow (follow conf-001/conf-002 as applicable).

Monetary amounts presented to the user (individual refunds/charges, per-order totals, aggregated totals, and price differences) MUST be rounded to two decimal places and shown in standard currency format when communicating and confirming actions. Compute price differences and net totals to the cent (rounding to nearest cent) before presenting them and before executing any tool calls that will charge or refund.

IF a user requests the tracking number(s) for an order (including delivered, returned, or cancelled orders) → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) if not already retrieved and present the order's fulfillment tracking_id(s) to the user. If tracking IDs are not available, inform the user that no tracking is available and that they will receive shipping/tracking updates by email if/when provided.

IF a user asks to compare financial outcomes (for example: 'Which option saves me more money?' between returning items and exchanging items/variants) → THEN gather the user's relevant order(s) via get_user_details(user_id) and get_order_details(order_id) for each involved order, identify the items referenced, call get_product_details(product_id) (and list_all_product_types if needed) to find candidate replacement variants and their available prices, compute exact per-item and aggregated refunds/charges (rounding to the nearest cent) for each option (return vs exchange or different exchange proposals), and present a clear comparison showing per-item impacts, totals, and which option results in the greatest savings. Require explicit user confirmation before executing any actions derived from that comparison.

IF a tool call fails due to a predictable validation error (for example: payment method mismatch, invalid order id, or insufficient balance) → THEN immediately inform the user of the error and its cause, present the correct policy or required value (for example: which payment method the system requires), and offer concrete options (retry with the original/correct payment method, supply an alternate valid payment method, pause the request, or escalate to a human). Do not retry the same tool call with a different/uncorroborated payment method or parameters without first confirming the user's choice.

IF any action tool is executed that changes orders or payments (modify_pending_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, cancel_pending_order) → THEN immediately call get_order_details(order_id) after the tool returns to verify the order's resulting items, status, fulfillments, and payment_history. Recompute exact per-item and per-order price differences and any refunds/charges to the cent from the updated order data, round monetary amounts to two decimal places, and present a clear per-order confirmation to the user including order_id, final item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (if any), per-item and per-order prices, the exact refund or charge amount(s) and the payment_method_id(s) used. If the post-action order details or payment_history do not match expected values, reconcile the discrepancy (re-query tools) or escalate to human agents before confirming completion to the user.

IF the user asks about alternative payment methods for any refund, charge, or price-difference associated with an exchange/modification/return → THEN: (a) ensure get_user_details(user_id) has been called and retrieve the user's payment_methods; (b) present the available payment methods to the user using friendly display labels (for example, 'Mastercard ending 1065', 'PayPal', 'Gift Card ending 7711863 (balance: $44.00)'); (c) indicate which payment method is the original payment method for the relevant order; (d) ask the user to choose which payment method to use for the refund/charge if they want to use one other than the original; (e) verify the chosen payment_method_id exists in get_user_details(payment_methods) before proceeding; (f) if a gift card is chosen for a charge, check its available balance and refuse the charge if insufficient (present the exact shortfall and the options per prec-007); and (g) require explicit user confirmation before applying charges/refunds to any payment method different from the order's original payment method.

IF a user requests an action (for example: return, exchange, tracking lookup, address-based update, or cancellation) for an item or order but does not provide a specific order_id → THEN: (a) obtain identifying info per info-001 and call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve the user's order IDs; (b) call get_order_details(order_id) for each order in the user's account as needed to locate the order(s) containing the referenced item(s) or shipping address; (c) present the found order_id(s) and matching item(s) (including item_id, product_id, options, price, status, and fulfillment/tracking where applicable) to the user and ask for confirmation of which order/item to act on; (d) if multiple matches or ambiguous references are found, prompt the user to disambiguate (by order_id, item_id, item options, or shipping address) before proposing or executing any account-specific action.

IF a single user message contains both general (non-account) product inquiries (browsing, specs, availability) AND account-specific requests (account/order changes, returns, exchanges, cancellations) → THEN handle the general product inquiries immediately without requesting identifying information, and separately request identifying information only for the account-specific actions. The assistant should clearly separate the two flows in its response (provide the product information first, then ask for the minimal identifying info required to look up or act on the account/orders and proceed with account lookups/actions per existing rules).

IF a refund/charge resulting from a return, exchange, or modification will be applied to a gift card (either the original payment method or another gift card in the account) → THEN include in the confirmation communication: (a) the exact refund or charge amount (rounded to two decimals) and the payment method id/name that will receive or be charged, (b) the updated gift card balance after the refund/charge (rounded to two decimals), and (c) the standard expected processing timeframe for the refund/charge (e.g., 'refund will take X–Y business days'). If the user asks when the gift card will be available, reiterate the standard timeframe and state that the assistant cannot guarantee expedited processing; offer escalation to human agents if the user requests further review.

final_policy:
## Information Gathering
  [info-001] IF a user requests account/order changes or returns/exchanges → THEN ask for identifying info: customer ID (user_id) OR email OR name and zip (at least one of these is required). Priority: if the user provides a customer ID/user_id, accept it and call get_user_details(user_id) immediately (do not require name/zip or email). If the user provides both name+zip and email, prefer find_user_id_by_email with the email; otherwise use find_user_id_by_name_zip with first_name, last_name, and zip before proceeding.
  [info-002] IF user provides name and zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip with first_name, last_name, and zip to get user_id
  [info-003] IF user provides email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email with the email to get user_id
  [info-004] IF user_id is obtained → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve email, payment methods, address, and list of order IDs
  [info-005] IF a specific order is referenced (order id given or found in user details) → THEN before taking any action on that order, call get_order_details(order_id) to retrieve the current order items, status, fulfillments, and payment_history. If get_order_details was already called earlier in the same conversation for that exact order and there have been no intervening state-changing actions, it may be reused; otherwise always re-call get_order_details. Do not execute exchange/return/modify/cancel tool calls unless a fresh or recent get_order_details has been obtained and confirms the order's current status and payment info.
  [info-006] IF a product variant or compatibility/option is needed to propose an exchange or modification OR the user supplies a numeric identifier that might be either a product_id or an item_id → THEN: (a) first try get_product_details(product_id=<provided_id>) when the user-provided id appears to be a product_id; (b) call list_all_product_types to find likely product_ids and call get_product_details(product_id) to inspect variant item_ids, options, availability, and price; (c) if get_product_details(product_id=<provided_id>) returns not found, search the product catalog's variants for a matching item_id (treating the provided id as a variant/item_id) and, if found, use the corresponding product_id and item_id for the proposed exchange/modification; (d) if the user provides an ordered list of acceptable option values for a given attribute (for example: "pressure: 8 bar, then 9 bar, then 7 bar"), search the product's variants for available==true variants matching the user's other fixed option constraints and then attempt to match the preferred attribute values in the user's specified order, selecting the first available variant that meets the first preference, or the next preference if none match, and so on; (e) if no variant matches any of the user's listed preferences, inform the user and offer alternatives (keep original item, choose a different attribute, or accept the closest available option) before proceeding; (f) if no match is found for any interpreted id or preference, ask the user to clarify whether the number they provided is a product id or an item id before proceeding.
  [info-007] IF an order contains multiple similar or identical items (duplicate item_ids or same product repeated) AND the user references 'one of them' or is ambiguous about which unit to act on → THEN ask the user to specify which item they mean (by item options, item_id index, shipping address, or other distinguishing detail) before proceeding with returns/exchanges/modifications.
  [info-008] IF find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) returns no user → THEN ask the user for their email and call find_user_id_by_email(email) to attempt to locate the account as a fallback.
  [info-009] IF a user provides a customer ID / user_id directly → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve the user's email, payment methods, address, and list of order IDs. Accept the provided user_id as valid identifying info in lieu of name+zip or email and DO NOT prompt for additional identifying information (name/zip/email) or repeat other lookups unless the user explicitly indicates they want to use a different account or believes the provided user_id is incorrect. If inconsistent identifying info is later provided and the user requests switching accounts, confirm explicitly before performing any account lookup or action on the alternate account.
  [info-010] IF a user requests an aggregate total or spending summary for a product type/category across their past orders → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain order IDs, call get_order_details(order_id) for each order, filter items by the requested product_id(s) or product name, sum the item prices, and present the total with an optional breakdown by order and item.
  [info-011] DO NOT request identifying information (name/zip/email) for general product inquiries, browsing, availability requests, or unrelated/entertainment questions (for example: poems, riddles, casual conversation). If the user's intent is ambiguous, first ask a short clarifying question to determine whether they are requesting an account-specific action; only prompt for identifying info when the user explicitly requests account-specific actions (account/order changes, returns, exchanges, cancellations, or other actions that require accessing the user's account or orders).
  [info-012] IF a user asks a question or makes a request that is unrelated to their account, orders, returns, or exchanges → THEN do not request identifying information (name/zip/email). Only request identifying info when it is needed to look up or act on the user's account/orders.
  [info-013] IF a user is unsure which payment method they used for an order → THEN always retrieve the relevant order_details and read payment_history to identify the original payment_method_id to use for refunds/charges before asking the user for a payment method. If the order_details include a usable payment_method_id, do not prompt the user for a payment method; instead state the identified original payment method to the user (for example: 'the original payment method was your Mastercard ending in 2478') and use it by default unless the user explicitly requests a different payment method. Only ask the user to provide or choose a payment method if the order details do not contain usable payment information or if the user explicitly requests a different payment method and the system permits it.
  [info-014] IF a user provides only a single name (for example, only a first name) together with a zip code → THEN ask for the full name (first and last) before attempting a name+zip lookup.
  [info-015] If a user asks how many product options/variants are currently available for a product or product type (for example, "how many t-shirt options are available?"), identify the product_id (use list_all_product_types if needed), call get_product_details(product_id), count the variants where available == true, and report that count to the user; optionally include a short summary of the available variants (key option attributes and prices) or how to view them. Do not request identifying information for this general product inquiry.
  [info-017] IF a user requests the "cheapest" variant matching specific criteria (for example: "cheapest i7 or better", "least expensive with 8GB RAM", etc.) → THEN call list_all_product_types and get_product_details(product_id), filter the product's variants to only those where available == true and whose options meet the user's specified criteria, select the matching variant(s) with the lowest price, and present the chosen variant(s) to the user including item_id, full options, availability, and price for explicit confirmation before proceeding. If multiple variants tie for lowest price, present all tied options and ask the user to choose.
  [info-018] IF a user asks to compare financial outcomes (for example: 'Which option saves me more money?' between returning items and exchanging items/variants) → THEN gather the user's relevant order(s) via get_user_details(user_id) and get_order_details(order_id) for each involved order, identify the items referenced, call get_product_details(product_id) (and list_all_product_types if needed) to find candidate replacement variants and their available prices, compute exact per-item and aggregated refunds/charges (rounding to the nearest cent) for each option (return vs exchange or different exchange proposals), and present a clear comparison showing per-item impacts, totals, and which option results in the greatest savings. Require explicit user confirmation before executing any actions derived from that comparison.
  [info-019] IF a user requests an action (for example: return, exchange, tracking lookup, address-based update, or cancellation) for an item or order but does not provide a specific order_id → THEN: (a) obtain identifying info per info-001 and call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve the user's order IDs; (b) call get_order_details(order_id) for each order in the user's account as needed to locate the order(s) containing the referenced item(s) or shipping address; (c) present the found order_id(s) and matching item(s) (including item_id, product_id, options, price, status, and fulfillment/tracking where applicable) to the user and ask for confirmation of which order/item to act on; (d) if multiple matches or ambiguous references are found, prompt the user to disambiguate (by order_id, item_id, item options, or shipping address) before proposing or executing any account-specific action.
  [info-020] IF a single user message contains both general (non-account) product inquiries (browsing, specs, availability) AND account-specific requests (account/order changes, returns, exchanges, cancellations) → THEN handle the general product inquiries immediately without requesting identifying information, and separately request identifying information only for the account-specific actions. The assistant should clearly separate the two flows in its response (provide the product information first, then ask for the minimal identifying info required to look up or act on the account/orders and proceed with account lookups/actions per existing rules).

## Action Execution
  [acti-001] IF order status is delivered AND user requests an exchange → THEN use exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id) to place the exchange
  [acti-002] IF order status is delivered AND user requests a return/refund → THEN use return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id) and set payment_method_id to the original payment method from the order's payment_history
  [acti-003] IF order status is pending AND user requests a size/variant change → THEN use modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id) to update the pending order
  [acti-004] IF order status is pending AND user requests cancellation → THEN use cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) to cancel and issue refund to the original payment method. Note: cancel_pending_order applies to the entire pending order; partial-item cancellations on pending orders are not supported. If the user requests cancelling only some items, inform them that partial cancellation of a pending order isn't available and offer alternatives (cancel the entire order, or modify/replace items if supported while pending).
  [acti-005] IF proposing a replacement item or variant → THEN select new_item_ids only from get_product_details variants where available == true
  [acti-006] IF a price difference exists for an exchange or modification → THEN charge or refund using the payment_method_id supplied (typically the original payment method) and include payment_method_id in the tool call
  [acti-007] IF a user explicitly requests refunds to a different payment method → THEN verify the requested payment_method_id belongs to the same user (exists in get_user_details). If it does and system policy permits applying refunds/charges to that method (e.g., for exchanges/price differences), honor the request. Refuse requests that attempt refunds to payment methods not in the user's account, to another customer's payment method, or to payment methods that the system does not support for refunds.
  [acti-008] IF a user requests an address change for their account and/or one or more orders → THEN present the exact new address that will be applied (or state 'use address on file' if the user explicitly requests that) and ask for explicit confirmation before executing. If the user provides only a partial or ambiguous address (for example: only city/state/zip or 'my NYC address'), do NOT assume or auto-fill a full address from the account; instead either (a) ask the user to provide the full address (address1, address2 if needed, city, state, country, zip) or (b) present the full address on file and explicitly ask the user to confirm that you should use that address. If the user asks to use an address from one of their orders, call get_order_details(order_id) to retrieve the shipping address; if the user explicitly instructs the assistant to use that order's address (clear, unambiguous authorization), you may proceed without re-presenting the address, otherwise present the retrieved shipping address and ask for explicit confirmation before making any changes. For account address updates call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip). For order updates, only modify orders with modifiable statuses (e.g., status 'pending') by calling modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) for each specified order. Confirm with the user which specific orders to change, perform the permitted updates, then report which orders were successfully updated and which could not be changed (e.g., already processed/delivered). If the user also requests the account default address be updated to match an order or the new address, update the account after confirming with the user. Finally, present the updated address(es) to the user and confirm completion.
  [acti-012] IF a user requests multiple changes (exchanges/modifications/returns) and only confirms some of the proposed replacements/options → THEN proceed only with the actions the user explicitly confirmed (execute tools for confirmed items) and leave unconfirmed items unchanged; confirm the exact actions and payment method for the confirmed subset before executing.
  [acti-013] IF order status is pending AND the user requests a shipping/address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the order's shipping address before any other pending-order actions or fulfillment; after updating, call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm the new address and any price/shipping changes, present the updated shipping details and order summary to the user, and ask for explicit confirmation. EXCEPTION: If the user has already provided an unambiguous, explicit authorization in the conversation to update the specified order address(es) (for example: the user explicitly says 'Please update my account and both orders to X address'), the assistant may proceed without an additional get_order_details/confirmation step.
  [acti-014] IF a user requests to split payment across multiple payment methods for a single order at the time of purchase → THEN refuse the request and inform the user that a single payment method must be used for that order; offer alternatives such as removing items to reduce the total, using a different single payment method, or placing a new separate order to use a different card. EXCEPTION: for order exchanges or modifications that create a price difference (additional charge or refund), it is permitted to apply the price difference to a different payment method than the original order (resulting in multiple payment entries in the order's payment_history). In those cases follow precondition rules for selecting and validating the payment_method_id and obtain explicit user confirmation before charging a different payment method unless the user already provided a clear authorization.
  [acti-017] IF a payment_method of type gift_card is used for a modification, pending-order charge, OR for a refund/return → THEN: (a) check and report the gift_card's available balance from get_user_details/payment method metadata before attempting to charge it; if the balance is insufficient to cover any required charge, DO NOT execute the exchange/modification and follow the shortfall process in prec-007; (b) when a refund is applied to a gift_card, update the gift_card balance appropriately (deduct for charges, add for refunds) in the system and include the updated gift card balance in the confirmation communication after the action is prepared and/or executed; (c) always state the expected standard processing timeframe for gift-card refunds/charges (e.g., 'refund will take X–Y business days') in the confirmation; and (d) report monetary amounts rounded to two decimal places and the payment_method_id used for the charge/refund. For charges, verify available balance per prec-007 before executing; for refunds, apply the refund to the gift card balance and include the updated balance and expected processing timeframe in the confirmation.
  [acti-019] IF a user requests refunds/returns be applied to a different payment method than the original order's payment_history (including requests for refunds on delivered items) → THEN: By default refund to the original payment method recorded in the order's payment_history and refuse requests to route such refunds to payment methods not in the user's account or to another customer's method. EXCEPTION: For exchanges or price-difference adjustments (including delivered-order exchanges and pending-order modifications) the customer may explicitly request that any charge or refund related to the exchange/modification be applied to a different payment method in their account; in those cases verify the requested payment_method_id belongs to the user (per prec-003) and, if the system supports it, honor the request and include payment_method_id in the tool call. Do not call return_delivered_order_items (or any refund tool) using an alternate payment method for standard returns of delivered items. If the requested alternate payment method cannot be used (for example, the original payment was a gift card) clearly explain why the refund cannot be applied to the requested method, present practical alternatives — (1) refund the specified returned item(s) to the original payment method only; (2) return the entire order so the full refund is processed to the original payment method; (3) perform an exchange or modification if that flow permits applying charges/refunds to a different payment method (verify ownership of that method per prec-003) and, if supported, apply the requested method; or (4) offer to escalate to human agents for review — and refuse any attempt to use a payment method not in the user's account while offering escalation. Require explicit user confirmation before proceeding with any chosen alternative; when the user selects an alternative, summarize the exact actions, the payment_method_id(s) that will be used, and any charges or refunds (rounded to cents) and obtain confirmation before calling the relevant tool(s).
  [acti-021] IF requested returns/exchanges include items from more than one order → THEN handle each order separately: call get_order_details(order_id) for each order involved, and for each order call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items using the payment_method_id from that order's payment_history for refunds/charges, ensuring each tool call references the correct order and payment method.
  [acti-026] IF a user requests an action that is disallowed by policy (for example, refunds to a different payment method) OR explicitly requests a human agent or escalation → THEN refuse the disallowed action per policy and offer/escalate to human agents using transfer_to_human_agents(summary) when the user requests it or when appropriate.
  [acti-027] IF a user requests updating or modifying the same product across all of their pending orders (for example: 'update all my pending t-shirt orders to X') → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain order IDs, call get_order_details(order_id) for each order, filter for orders with status 'pending' that contain the specified product_id or product name, prepare a per-order proposal listing order_id, current item_id(s) to be changed, proposed new_item_id, per-item price and any price differences, and the payment_method_id that will be charged/refunded for that order (use that order's original payment method). Present the aggregated proposal and require explicit user confirmation before executing modify_pending_order_items for each eligible pending order. Do not modify non-pending orders and inform the user which orders (if any) were ineligible.
  [acti-028] IF an order contains multiple identical items represented by duplicate item_ids AND the user requests exchanges/returns affecting one or more units → THEN treat each unit as a separate item by including the item_id multiple times in tool calls (e.g., ["id","id"]) to indicate quantity. If the user references units ambiguously (for example: 'one of them' or 'both'), ask the user to specify which units or to confirm the number of units to act on before proceeding. Ensure get_order_details has been called to confirm item_ids and quantities before any exchange/return tool calls.
  [acti-029] IF a user requests cancelling all (or multiple) pending orders at once → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) and get_order_details(order_id) for each order to identify which orders are currently in 'pending' status. Present the list of pending orders (order_id and items) to the user and ask whether they want to cancel all pending orders or select specific ones, and request a cancellation reason (a single reason can be applied to multiple orders). After explicit user confirmation and reason, call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) for each pending order selected, report which orders were successfully cancelled, and provide refund details (payment_method used and amounts). If the user did not provide a reason, ask for one prior to cancelling. If some orders are not pending, inform the user those cannot be cancelled and offer alternatives.
  [acti-031] IF a user requests lowering a pending order's total to a target amount by swapping item variants → THEN for each item call get_product_details(product_id) to identify available (available == true) variants and select the lowest-priced available variant; propose replacements only for items where a cheaper available variant exists; compute the new per-item prices, per-order total, and the net refund or charge; present a clear per-item and per-order breakdown (order_id, old item_ids and prices, proposed new_item_ids and prices, net price difference, and the payment_method_id that will be used) and ask for explicit confirmation before calling modify_pending_order_items. If the cheapest available replacements cannot meet the user's target total, inform the user and offer alternatives (remove items, choose different variants, or cancel the order).
  [acti-033] IF a tool call fails due to a predictable validation error (for example: payment method mismatch, invalid order id, or insufficient balance) → THEN immediately inform the user of the error and its cause, present the correct policy or required value (for example: which payment method the system requires), and offer concrete options (retry with the original/correct payment method, supply an alternate valid payment method, pause the request, or escalate to a human). Do not retry the same tool call with a different/uncorroborated payment method or parameters without first confirming the user's choice.
  [acti-034] IF any action tool is executed that changes orders or payments (modify_pending_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, cancel_pending_order) → THEN immediately call get_order_details(order_id) after the tool returns to verify the order's resulting items, status, fulfillments, and payment_history. Recompute exact per-item and per-order price differences and any refunds/charges to the cent from the updated order data, round monetary amounts to two decimal places, and present a clear per-order confirmation to the user including order_id, final item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (if any), per-item and per-order prices, the exact refund or charge amount(s) and the payment_method_id(s) used. If the post-action order details or payment_history do not match expected values, reconcile the discrepancy (re-query tools) or escalate to human agents before confirming completion to the user.
  [acti-035] IF the user asks about alternative payment methods for any refund, charge, or price-difference associated with an exchange/modification/return → THEN: (a) ensure get_user_details(user_id) has been called and retrieve the user's payment_methods; (b) present the available payment methods to the user using friendly display labels (for example, 'Mastercard ending 1065', 'PayPal', 'Gift Card ending 7711863 (balance: $44.00)'); (c) indicate which payment method is the original payment method for the relevant order; (d) ask the user to choose which payment method to use for the refund/charge if they want to use one other than the original; (e) verify the chosen payment_method_id exists in get_user_details(payment_methods) before proceeding; (f) if a gift card is chosen for a charge, check its available balance and refuse the charge if insufficient (present the exact shortfall and the options per prec-007); and (g) require explicit user confirmation before applying charges/refunds to any payment method different from the order's original payment method.

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF preparing to execute a change (exchange, return, modification, cancellation) → THEN present the exact actions to the user including order id, old item_ids, proposed new item_ids (or return/cancel), price(s), and payment_method_id to be used and ask for explicit confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue'). If the user has already provided an unambiguous, explicit instruction authorizing the requested change earlier in the conversation (for example, they asked to proceed with the specific exchange/modification and specified preferences), restate the exact actions being taken and proceed without an additional 'Yes' only if the prior message clearly authorized proceeding.
  [conf-002] IF an exchange/return results in a price difference → THEN communicate the exact refund or charge amount and the payment method it will be applied to (for refunds: 'refund would be $X to your [payment method]') after executing the action
  [conf-003] IF an order will ship later → THEN tell the user they will receive an email when it ships
  [conf-004] AFTER completing requested actions via tools → THEN send a short confirmation message (e.g., 'Done!', 'All done') and ask if anything else is needed
  [conf-005] IF the user requests multiple returns/exchanges across multiple orders → THEN compute and clearly present the aggregated total refund or total charge (net) and list which payment method(s) each amount will be applied to (e.g., 'Total refund would be $X to your [payment method(s)]'), and confirm with the user.
  [conf-006] IF multiple exchanges/returns/modifications are requested across one or more orders → THEN present a per-order breakdown listing order_id, old item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or return/cancel), per-item and per-order price(s), the price difference for each order, the payment_method_id to be used for each charge/refund, and the total net charge or refund across all orders; require explicit user confirmation before executing any tool calls. EXCEPTION: If the assistant provides a clear, unambiguous summary of the proposed replacements/changes and the user explicitly confirms (for example 'Please proceed' or equivalent), the assistant may proceed without repeating a full per-order line-item breakdown.
  [conf-007] IF cancelling a pending order → THEN ask for the cancellation reason (e.g., 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') prior to cancelling. A clear user instruction with a stated reason may be treated as confirmation to proceed with the cancellation.
  [conf-008] IF a user returns delivered items (initiates a return/refund) → THEN inform the user of the expected refund processing time (for example, 'refund will take X–Y business days') in the confirmation communication along with the amount and the payment method that will receive the refund.
  [conf-009] Monetary amounts presented to the user (individual refunds/charges, per-order totals, aggregated totals, and price differences) MUST be rounded to two decimal places and shown in standard currency format when communicating and confirming actions. Compute price differences and net totals to the cent (rounding to nearest cent) before presenting them and before executing any tool calls that will charge or refund.
  [conf-010] IF a user requests the tracking number(s) for an order (including delivered, returned, or cancelled orders) → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) if not already retrieved and present the order's fulfillment tracking_id(s) to the user. If tracking IDs are not available, inform the user that no tracking is available and that they will receive shipping/tracking updates by email if/when provided.
  [conf-011] IF a refund/charge resulting from a return, exchange, or modification will be applied to a gift card (either the original payment method or another gift card in the account) → THEN include in the confirmation communication: (a) the exact refund or charge amount (rounded to two decimals) and the payment method id/name that will receive or be charged, (b) the updated gift card balance after the refund/charge (rounded to two decimals), and (c) the standard expected processing timeframe for the refund/charge (e.g., 'refund will take X–Y business days'). If the user asks when the gift card will be available, reiterate the standard timeframe and state that the assistant cannot guarantee expedited processing; offer escalation to human agents if the user requests further review.

## Precondition Rules
  [prec-001] IF an action involves returned or exchanged items → THEN verify the order's status: delivered orders may be returned or exchanged; pending orders must be modified or cancelled; cancelled orders cannot be returned or exchanged. (Do not use delivered-return/exchange tools on pending or cancelled orders.)
  [prec-002] IF modifying/exchanging an item → THEN ensure the replacement variant's availability is true in get_product_details before proposing it
  [prec-003] IF performing any charge or refund → THEN ensure the specified payment_method_id exists in get_user_details(payment_methods). Default to the original payment method from the order's payment_history for refunds and for any additional charges arising from exchanges/modifications unless the customer explicitly requests a different payment method or the original method lacks sufficient funds. If the original payment method is of type gift_card, check its available balance (from get_user_details/payment method metadata) before attempting to charge it; if the balance is sufficient, use the gift card by default. If the original method lacks sufficient funds, inform the user of the exact shortfall and offer options: (a) provide an alternate payment method, (b) charge the remainder to a different payment method (with the user's explicit confirmation), (c) pause/hold the requested change until the user supplies payment, or (d) cancel the change request. Require explicit user confirmation before charging a payment method different from the original unless the user has already given an unambiguous authorization to proceed with that different method.
  [prec-004] IF no explicit user confirmation is received after a proposed change → THEN do not execute exchange/return/modify/cancel actions. EXCEPTION: treat an unambiguous user instruction to perform the action (for example: 'Cancel this order', 'No longer needed', 'Please modify to X') or a clearly stated cancellation reason as sufficient confirmation to proceed without an additional 'Yes' prompt.
  [prec-005] IF a user requests returns and exchanges in the same request → THEN do not execute both actions simultaneously; ask the user to choose which action to perform first (process them as separate requests). EXCEPTION: If the requested returns and exchanges apply to different orders, the assistant may handle each order separately without asking the user to pick which to perform first—treat each order as its own request, call get_order_details(order_id) for each involved order, and follow per-order confirmation and execution rules (e.g., acti-021 and conf-006).
  [prec-006] IF changing a user's account address → THEN note that this does NOT retroactively change the shipping address on already processed or delivered orders; order shipping addresses must be updated separately and only pending/processed (modifiable) orders can have their shipping address updated. Changing or reverting the account address also does NOT automatically change order-level shipping addresses (including pending orders) unless the user explicitly requests that specific orders be updated. Treat the account-level address and each order's shipping address as separate entities: require explicit user authorization to modify order addresses (per-order or for all pending orders), follow the rules for pending-order address modification (acti-013) when eligible, and always inform the user which orders (by order id) were updated and which orders could not be updated due to status.
  [prec-007] IF an exchange or modification requires charging a payment method that may have limited balance (e.g., a gift card) → THEN check the payment_method's available balance (from get_user_details/payment method metadata) before executing. If the balance is insufficient to cover any required charge, DO NOT execute the exchange/modification. Inform the user of the insufficient balance, provide the exact shortfall amount, and offer options: (a) provide an alternate payment method, (b) pause/hold the requested change until the user supplies payment, or (c) cancel the change request. Always use the original payment method for refunds.
  [prec-008] IF an order's status is cancelled → THEN the order cannot be reinstated via automated return/modify/cancel/exchange tools; refuse automated reinstatement and escalate to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents) for manual review/possible reinstatement.
  [prec-009] IF an order status is pending AND the user requests cancellation of only some items in that pending order → THEN do not perform partial cancellations; pending orders may be modified (change variants/quantities) using modify_pending_order_items or cancelled in full using cancel_pending_order. Partial-item returns/cancellations are only allowed for delivered orders.
  [prec-010] IF an order already has an active return_requested or exchange_requested state → THEN do not initiate additional return or exchange requests for the same order until the existing request is resolved; inform the user of the existing request and next steps.
  [prec-011] IF a user requests cancellation for only part of a pending order → THEN inform the user that partial cancellations are not supported (or per-system policy), and offer the appropriate alternative (use modify_pending_order_items to remove/replace items if supported, or cancel the entire order if the system supports it).
  [prec-012] IF an address modification request is received → THEN check the order status: delivered orders' shipping addresses cannot be changed; only pending/processed (modifiable) orders can have their shipping address updated. Inform the user which orders cannot be changed and proceed only for eligible orders.
  [prec-013] DO NOT guarantee expedited refund processing outside the standard stated processing window. If a user requests a faster refund timeframe than standard (for example, 'within 3 days'), state the standard processing timeframe (e.g., 'refunds typically take 5–7 business days'), explain that the assistant cannot ensure an accelerated processing time, and offer to escalate to human agents for review if the customer insists or requests further assistance (use transfer_to_human_agents(summary)).
  [prec-014] IF a user requests a return/refund but indicates they do not have the physical item (lost/stolen/missing) → THEN refuse to process an automated return because returns require possession of the item. Explain that an automated return cannot be completed without the item, provide the order's tracking information if available, and offer alternatives: (a) escalate/file a claim for investigation with human agents (use transfer_to_human_agents(summary)) if the user requests further review, (b) place a new order if the user wants a replacement, or (c) advise on next steps for recovery with the carrier. If the user insists on a refund despite the item being missing, offer to escalate to human agents for manual review rather than performing an automated refund.
  [prec-015] IF a user requests returns/exchanges that include items belonging to orders with status 'cancelled' → THEN do not attempt automated returns/exchanges for those cancelled items. Inform the user which specific items and order_id(s) are cancelled and cannot be returned, proceed with return/exchange actions only for eligible items from other orders (call get_order_details for each order and use the appropriate return/exchange tool per order), and offer to escalate/transfer to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents(summary)) if the user requests review or possible reinstatement of cancelled orders/items.
  [prec-016] IF an exchange requests replacement of a delivered item with the same item_id (a like-for-like replacement) → THEN allow exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id) where new_item_ids equal the original item_ids, provided get_product_details confirms the variant is available (or availability is not applicable for direct replacements), there is no existing active return_requested or exchange_requested for the same order, and the user has explicitly requested the replacement. Treat this as a standard exchange/replacement flow (follow conf-001/conf-002 as applicable).

If the order_details include a usable payment_method_id, do not prompt the user for a payment method; instead state the identified original payment method to the user (for example: 'the original payment method was your Mastercard ending in 2478') and use it by default unless the user explicitly requests a different payment method. Only ask the user to provide or choose a payment method if the order details do not contain usable payment information or if the user explicitly requests a different payment method and the system permits it.

actions.old_content: IF a user requests refunds/returns to be applied to a different payment method than the original order's payment_history → THEN: For standard refunds from returns (i.e., returning delivered items), refund to the original payment method from the order's payment_history by default and refuse requests to route such refunds to payment methods not in the user's account or to another customer's method. EXCEPTION: For exchanges or price-difference adjustments (including delivered-order exchanges and pending-order modifications) the customer may explicitly request that any charge or refund related to the exchange/modification be applied to a different payment method in their account; in those cases verify the requested payment_method_id belongs to the user (per prec-003) and, if the system supports it, honor the request and include payment_method_id in the tool call. If the requested alternate payment method is not in the user's account or is otherwise disallowed, refuse and offer to escalate to a human agent.
actions.new_content: IF a user requests refunds/returns to be applied to a different payment method than the original order's payment_history → THEN: By default refund to the original payment method from the order's payment_history and refuse requests to route such refunds to payment methods not in the user's account or to another customer's method. EXCEPTION: For exchanges or price-difference adjustments (including delivered-order exchanges and pending-order modifications) the customer may explicitly request that any charge or refund related to the exchange/modification be applied to a different payment method in their account; in those cases verify the requested payment_method_id belongs to the user (per prec-003) and, if the system supports it, honor the request and include payment_method_id in the tool call. If a user requests a refund to a different payment method for a standard return of delivered items and the system cannot refund to the requested method (for example, the original payment for that order was a gift card), clearly explain why the refund cannot be applied to the requested method, present practical alternatives (for example: (a) refund the returned item(s) to the original payment method only; (b) return the entire order so the full refund goes to the original payment method; (c) perform an exchange/modification that permits routing differences when supported), and offer escalation to human agents if the user insists. Proceed only after explicit user confirmation for the chosen alternative. If the requested alternate payment method is not in the user's account or is otherwise disallowed, refuse and offer to escalate to a human agent.

actions.old_content: IF a payment_method of type gift_card is used for a modification, pending-order charge, OR for a refund/return → THEN update the gift_card balance appropriately (deduct for charges, add for refunds), update the user's payment_methods balance in get_user_details, and report the resulting gift card balance to the user as part of the confirmation communication after the action is prepared and/or executed. For charges, verify available balance per prec-007 before executing; for refunds, apply the refund to the gift card balance and include the updated balance in the confirmation.
actions.new_content: IF a payment_method of type gift_card is used for a modification, pending-order charge, OR for a refund/return → THEN: (a) check and report the gift_card's available balance from get_user_details/payment method metadata before attempting to charge it; if the balance is insufficient to cover any required charge, DO NOT execute the exchange/modification and follow the shortfall process in prec-007; (b) when a refund is applied to a gift_card, update the gift_card balance appropriately (deduct for charges, add for refunds) in the system and include the updated gift card balance in the confirmation communication after the action is prepared and/or executed; (c) always state the expected standard processing timeframe for gift-card refunds/charges (e.g., 'refund will take X–Y business days') in the confirmation; and (d) report monetary amounts rounded to two decimal places and the payment_method_id used for the charge/refund. For charges, verify available balance per prec-007 before executing; for refunds, apply the refund to the gift card balance and include the updated balance and expected processing timeframe in the confirmation.

actions.content: IF a user asks to compare financial outcomes (for example: 'Which option saves me more money?' between returning items and exchanging items/variants) → THEN gather the user's relevant order(s) via get_user_details(user_id) and get_order_details(order_id) for each involved order, identify the items referenced, call get_product_details(product_id) (and list_all_product_types if needed) to find candidate replacement variants and their available prices, compute exact per-item and aggregated refunds/charges (rounding to the nearest cent) for each option (return vs exchange or different exchange proposals), and present a clear comparison showing per-item impacts, totals, and which option results in the greatest savings. Require explicit user confirmation before executing any actions derived from that comparison.

IF a user requests a refund/return for delivered items and asks that the refund be applied to a different payment method than the original payment recorded in the order's payment_history → THEN do NOT call return_delivered_order_items using the alternate payment method. Instead inform the user that standard refunds for returned/delivered items are issued to the original payment method by default, explain the exception rules (exchanges/price-differences may be applied to a different payment method only if it belongs to the user's account and the user gives explicit authorization), offer alternatives (refund to original method, escalate to human agents to request an exception, or provide guidance for other options), and ask the user to confirm using the original payment method before making any tool call to initiate the refund.

IF a tool call fails due to a predictable validation error (for example: payment method mismatch, invalid order id, or insufficient balance) → THEN immediately inform the user of the error and its cause, present the correct policy or required value (for example: which payment method the system requires), and offer concrete options (retry with the original/correct payment method, supply an alternate valid payment method, pause the request, or escalate to a human). Do not retry the same tool call with a different/uncorroborated payment method or parameters without first confirming the user's choice.

IF any action tool is executed that changes orders or payments (modify_pending_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, cancel_pending_order) → THEN immediately call get_order_details(order_id) after the tool returns to verify the order's resulting items, status, fulfillments, and payment_history. Recompute exact per-item and per-order price differences and any refunds/charges to the cent from the updated order data, round monetary amounts to two decimal places, and present a clear per-order confirmation to the user including order_id, final item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (if any), per-item and per-order prices, the exact refund or charge amount(s) and the payment_method_id(s) used. If the post-action order details or payment_history do not match expected values, reconcile the discrepancy (re-query tools) or escalate to human agents before confirming completion to the user.

IF the user asks about alternative payment methods for any refund, charge, or price-difference associated with an exchange/modification/return → THEN: (a) ensure get_user_details(user_id) has been called and retrieve the user's payment_methods; (b) present the available payment methods to the user using friendly display labels (for example, 'Mastercard ending 1065', 'PayPal', 'Gift Card ending 7711863 (balance: $44.00)'); (c) indicate which payment method is the original payment method for the relevant order; (d) ask the user to choose which payment method to use for the refund/charge if they want to use one other than the original; (e) verify the chosen payment_method_id exists in get_user_details(payment_methods) before proceeding; (f) if a gift card is chosen for a charge, check its available balance and refuse the charge if insufficient (present the exact shortfall and the options per prec-007); and (g) require explicit user confirmation before applying charges/refunds to any payment method different from the order's original payment method.

IF a user requests an action (for example: return, exchange, tracking lookup, address-based update, or cancellation) for an item or order but does not provide a specific order_id → THEN: (a) obtain identifying info per info-001 and call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve the user's order IDs; (b) call get_order_details(order_id) for each order in the user's account as needed to locate the order(s) containing the referenced item(s) or shipping address; (c) present the found order_id(s) and matching item(s) (including item_id, product_id, options, price, status, and fulfillment/tracking where applicable) to the user and ask for confirmation of which order/item to act on; (d) if multiple matches or ambiguous references are found, prompt the user to disambiguate (by order_id, item_id, item options, or shipping address) before proposing or executing any account-specific action.

IF a single user message contains both general (non-account) product inquiries (browsing, specs, availability) AND account-specific requests (account/order changes, returns, exchanges, cancellations) → THEN handle the general product inquiries immediately without requesting identifying information, and separately request identifying information only for the account-specific actions. The assistant should clearly separate the two flows in its response (provide the product information first, then ask for the minimal identifying info required to look up or act on the account/orders and proceed with account lookups/actions per existing rules).

IF a user requests a refund to a different payment method than the order's original payment and that request cannot be honored → THEN, after explaining the limitation, proactively present and offer clear alternatives and require explicit confirmation before proceeding: (1) return only the specified item(s) and refund to the original payment method; (2) return the entire order so the entire refund is processed to the original payment method; (3) propose an exchange or modification if that flow permits applying charges/refunds to a different payment method (verify ownership of that method per prec-003); or (4) offer to escalate to human agents for review. When the user selects an alternative, summarize the exact actions, the payment_method_id(s) that will be used, and any charges or refunds (rounded to cents) and obtain confirmation before calling the relevant tool(s).

IF a refund/charge resulting from a return, exchange, or modification will be applied to a gift card (either the original payment method or another gift card in the account) → THEN include in the confirmation communication: (a) the exact refund or charge amount (rounded to two decimals) and the payment method id/name that will receive or be charged, (b) the updated gift card balance after the refund/charge (rounded to two decimals), and (c) the standard expected processing timeframe for the refund/charge (e.g., 'refund will take X–Y business days'). If the user asks when the gift card will be available, reiterate the standard timeframe and state that the assistant cannot guarantee expedited processing; offer escalation to human agents if the user requests further review.

events.merged_content: IF a user requests refunds/returns be applied to a different payment method than the original order's payment_history (including requests for refunds on delivered items) → THEN: By default refund to the original payment method recorded in the order's payment_history and refuse requests to route such refunds to payment methods not in the user's account or to another customer's method. EXCEPTION: For exchanges or price-difference adjustments (including delivered-order exchanges and pending-order modifications) the customer may explicitly request that any charge or refund related to the exchange/modification be applied to a different payment method in their account; in those cases verify the requested payment_method_id belongs to the user (per prec-003) and, if the system supports it, honor the request and include payment_method_id in the tool call. Do not call return_delivered_order_items (or any refund tool) using an alternate payment method for standard returns of delivered items. If the requested alternate payment method cannot be used (for example, the original payment was a gift card) clearly explain why the refund cannot be applied to the requested method, present practical alternatives — (1) refund the specified returned item(s) to the original payment method only; (2) return the entire order so the full refund is processed to the original payment method; (3) perform an exchange or modification if that flow permits applying charges/refunds to a different payment method (verify ownership of that method per prec-003) and, if supported, apply the requested method; or (4) offer to escalate to human agents for review — and refuse any attempt to use a payment method not in the user's account while offering escalation. Require explicit user confirmation before proceeding with any chosen alternative; when the user selects an alternative, summarize the exact actions, the payment_method_id(s) that will be used, and any charges or refunds (rounded to cents) and obtain confirmation before calling the relevant tool(s).
